data.title: CZ’s family office YZi Labs ‘deepens’ support of Ethena Labs, weeks after Binance adds USDe integrations
data.description:
YZi Labs, the family office created with former Binance CEO Changpeng Zhao, is increasing its stake in Ethena Labs, the startup behind the popular USDe synthetic dollar. The move comes as Ethena looks to "expand its presence" on BNB Chain, including supporting new money markets, protocol integrations, and ecosystem partnerships.

"We're thrilled to deepen our partnership with YZi Labs, a long-standing and strategic backer," Ethena CEO Guy Young said in a statement. "The holy grail of digital dollar distribution has always been embedding stable, yield-bearing assets directly into the core of the crypto economy."

YZi Labs (formerly Binance Labs) was one of Ethena’s earliest backers, selecting it as one of 12 early-stage projects to support during the venture firm’s “Incubation Season 6,” before USDe’s official launch.

USDe debuted in early 2024 as a sort of onchain bond that maintained its peg to the dollar using a "delta-neutral" hedging strategy, unlike any other stablecoin at the time. The project quickly gained support from notable investors, including Arthur Hayes' family office Maelstrom and venture firm Dragonfly, as well as major corporations like Brevan Howard and Franklin Templeton.

Ethena's USDe was the fastest onchain greenback alternative to cross the $10 billion milestone, and currently has a market cap and circulating supply of around $14 billion, making it the third-largest “stablecoin,” according to The Block’s data. The asset was first extended to BNB Chain in April.

YZi’s announcement on Friday does not clarify exactly how CZ's family office "has deepened its holding" in Ethena Labs or whether this includes additional financing. It notes "on the product front," YZi’s support will enable the startup to "further drive USDe adoption across both CEXs and DeFi" and advance its USDtb stablecoin, which is fully-backed by BlackRock’s BUIDL fund.

Ethena is co-developing Converge, an EVM-based and institutionally-geared settlement layer developed with Securitize and several BlackRock tokenization partners "to bring TradFi-grade RWAs on-chain."

Earlier this month, Binance, the world’s largest crypto exchange, announced it would integrate USDe as a spot trading pair and yield-bearing collateral asset for derivatives traders.

YZi Labs, now a $10 billion family office, was spun out of Binance in January, months after Zhao was released from prison. The former Binance CEO served a four-month sentence in 2024, having pleaded guilty in 2023 to charges related to violating anti-money laundering laws and other compliance failures.

data.title: Peru central bank raises 2025 growth forecast
data.description:
Peru's central bank on Friday slightly raised its economic growth forecast for 2025 to 3.2%, from 3.1% previously, citing stronger-than-expected non-primary sector activity and private spending.

The bank also projected annual inflation at 1.7% by the end of 2025 and 2.0% by the end of 2026, supported by the fading impact of supply shocks and economic activity operating near its potential level.

Annual inflation in August eased to 1.11%, the lowest in seven years, bringing it closer to the lower end of the central bank's 1% to 3% target range.

The central bank also forecast on its report a fiscal deficit of 2.4% of gross domestic product (GDP) in 2025 and 2.1% in 2026.

data.title: Striking machinists at Boeing Defense approve union's proposed contract that company rejected
data.description:
Key points:
  • IAM union will send approved offer to Boeing Defense
  • Boeing management refused to consider union-proposed contract
  • Union seeks larger bonus, retirement contributions, wage hikes

By Dan Catchpole

Striking workers at Boeing Defense approved a four-year contract proposal from union leadership that management has already refused to consider, the International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers (IAM) announced Friday.

On Wednesday, Boeing Defense leadership dismissed the offer, which was unilaterally proposed after talks stopped, as a "publicity stunt." The company has said it would hire replacement workers to assemble munitions, fighters and other military aircraft in the St. Louis area.

The IAM's proposed offer builds on earlier proposals from Boeing Defense, adding a larger ratification bonus than management offered, heftier company contributions to the retirement plan and steeper wage increases for workers at the hourly wage ceiling.

Union officials said they drafted the proposal because the company has not been willing to resume talks since the roughly 3,200 members of IAM District 837 voted 57% to reject Boeing's last offer on September 12.

data.title: Trump and Xi Discuss TikTok, Trade Tensions in Call. What Else We Know. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Reshma Kapadia and Angela Palumbo

President Donald Trump said in a Truth Social post that he completed a "very productive call," with Chinese President Xi Jinping on Friday.

"We made progress on many very important issues including Trade, Fentanyl, the need to bring the War between Russia and Ukraine to an end, and the approval of the TikTok Deal," Trump said in a post on Truth Social on Friday.

The details surrounding the TikTok deal are still unknown.

This is breaking news. Read a preview of the Trump-Xi call below and check back for more analysis soon.

An expected phone call Friday between President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping, is a precursor to a meeting between the men that could yield a broader trade deal.

After an escalation in tensions in April that led to triple-digit tariffs and restrictions on critical minerals access for the U.S. and technology and other goods for China, the two sides have tried to stabilize the relationship, paving the way for an eventual meeting.

Even as Beijing said recently that U.S. tech giant Nvidia had violated its antitrust laws, it closed a probe into Google. Similarly, the Trump team has talked about the respectful nature of discussions of late with their Chinese counterparts and allowed Nvidia to sell its H20 chips in China.

On Monday, U.S. trade negotiators reached a framework agreement with their Chinese counterparts for ByteDance to divest its social media app TikTok to U.S. owners. Details weren't disclosed, and the deal was subject to the approval of both leaders.

Most geopolitical analysts expect a Trump-Xi call to produce a further softening of rhetoric. While export restrictions, tariffs and Chinese agreements to buy soybeans or invest in the U.S. will likely be part of discussions, analysts don't expect much movement on this front. The focus will be in getting an face-to-face leader meeting that could produce a deal on these fronts.

One concern is if the two sides walk away with different versions of what was agreed upon. Misunderstandings earlier in the summer over U.S. access to rare minerals rattled auto and industrial companies reliant on China for critical magnets.

That concern stems in part from the different readouts of the call on Monday: the U.S. team said the conversation was mostly focused on TikTok while China said that it was a broader discussion related to reducing investment barriers and promoting economic and trade ties. Analysts note that it isn't clear what the U.S. is giving to China as a concession for TikTok.

"It will be good to see the degree to which they are coordinated and if they agree on what they are talking about with the TikTok deal. The call will be illuminating on that front," says Henrietta Levin, senior fellow at the Center for Strategic and International Studies in Washington and a former director for China at the White House National Security Council. "The fact they both want a call and a meeting suggests tactical stability in the relationship, but it doesn't mean they will get it."

The framework agreement calls for a U.S. consortium including Oracle to own 80% of TikTok, with the new company possibly licensing ByteDance's technology and then re-creating the algorithm that has made the app so valuable , according to press reports. The data would be managed by Oracle, which has been in a partnership with TikTok to do so. The licensing agreement doesn't seem to address the national security concerns that led to the rule that the algorithm couldn't be owned by a Chinese entity. But analysts said that there is room for such a deal to go through if the president approves it. Any deal would still need approval by the Chinese government, which has restrictions on the export of technology. "Xi sees that Trump wants TikTok and this can bring him to the table for a broader negotiation," says Laila Khawaja, head of technology research at Gavekal, who adds that TikTok isn't that important to Beijing. ByteDance is a successful technology company that gets about a quarter of its revenue overseas — and less than 20% from TikTok in the U.S., where it is a major source of revenue for content creators and small businesses. If the TikTok deal is successful, Gavekal analysts said in a press briefing that it could pave the way for the Trump and Xi to keep de-escalating. The TikTok deal could also offer a structure for future operations where a U.S. majority owned company could license Chinese technology. It could offer a path to revive the licensing deal Ford had with battery maker CATL that was put on hold due to national security concerns, the analysts say. However, they said it was unlikely that Chinese EV giant BYD would gain access to the U.S. market because it poses such a threat to domestic auto makers.

Trump has voiced an openness to China investing in the U.S., but his America First Investment policy restricts such investments. In addition to a lifting of the restrictions on China's access to technology, China also wants to see restrictions loosened on its inbound investments. If the Trump administration takes that path, it could build on de-escalating tensions between the two geopolitical rivals. That's what the market is betting on.

Write to Reshma Kapadia at reshma.kapadia@barrons.com

data.title: US Stocks Eye for Fresh Highs
data.description:
Wall Street’s main indexes were trading at record highs in Friday’s afternoon session, as investors weighed upbeat corporate earnings, the Fed’s first rate cut of 2025, and signs of progress in US-China relations.

The S&P 500 rose 0.3% and the Nasdaq 100 gained 0.5%, while the Dow added more than 100 points, extending the all-time highs set in the previous session.

FedEx climbed nearly 3% after posting stronger-than-expected results, while Apple jumped 2.8% on a J.P. Morgan price target hike and the launch of its new iPhone.

Tesla advanced 1.5% following an upgrade to “outperform” from Baird, helping lift the tech and consumer discretionary sectors.

Meanwhile, markets tracked a lengthy call between President Trump and China’s Xi Jinping, with Trump highlighting progress on trade, fentanyl, and a potential TikTok deal.

Despite choppy moves, all three major indexes remain on course for solid weekly gains, underscoring the strength of the ongoing rally.

data.title: Jimmy Kimmel suspension puts Disney back in hotseat; writers hold protest
data.description:
Film and TV writers held a free-speech demonstration in New York on Friday to protest Disney's suspension of talk-show host Jimmy Kimmel and President Donald Trump's efforts to punish broadcasters who speak ill of him or his murdered ally, the right-wing activist Charlie Kirk.

ABC, the television network owned by the Walt Disney Co , indefinitely suspended "Jimmy Kimmel Live!" this week after Kimmel discussed Kirk and mocked the president during his opening monologue on Monday's show.

Some conservatives were outraged by the monologue. The Trump-appointed head of the Federal Communications Commission threatened regulatory action, alarming Democrats and civil rights groups who accused the Trump administration of unconstitutional efforts to censor critical media. The owners of many ABC-affiliated local TV networks said they would not carry the show until Kimmel apologized to Kirk's family.

Disney has been targeted by conservatives before, particularly in a bruising political battle in 2022 over a Florida law that limited classroom discussion of sexual orientation or gender identity. The entertainment giant's initial response angered many of its employees, which pushed then-CEO Bob Chapek to take a more critical stance that collided with Florida Governor Ron DeSantis, a Republican.

On Friday in Manhattan, dozens of film and TV writers, including some employed by ABC, marched on the sidewalk outside the downtown skyscraper where Disney has its offices to "protect free speech" in a rally organized by their labor union, the Writers Guild of America.

In a rally organized by WGA in Los Angeles on Thursday, about 150 demonstrators protested outside the Hollywood studio where "Jimmy Kimmel Live!" is recorded, waving signs saying: "Don't Bend a Knee to Trump"; "Resist fascism" and "Douse the mouse."

Trump, a former successful TV host himself, spoke several times during a state visit to Britain this week to commend Kimmel's suspension, calling the Los Angeles comedian untalented and denouncing him for saying "a horrible thing about a great gentleman known as Charlie Kirk."

Kimmel, who frequently lampoons Trump, said in his monologue that Kirk's allies were using the onstage murder of the 31-year-old activist last week to "score political points." Kimmel also mocked Trump for turning a question about his grief for Kirk into a cheerful promotion for his planned White House ballroom.

"This is not how an adult grieves the murder of someone he called a friend," Kimmel said. "This is how a four-year-old mourns a goldfish."

Kimmel has not publicly commented since his suspension, and the future of his show remains unclear.

Trump also complained to reporters as he flew back to the U.S. that television networks "were 97% against me" and only gave him bad publicity.

"I would think maybe their license should be taken away," Trump said. Federal law prohibits the FCC from revoking a broadcaster's license for negative coverage or other speech disliked by the government.

Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, a Democrat, said Trump should fire FCC Chairman Brendan Carr, calling him "one of the single greatest threats to free speech America has ever known."

"We will not stand by and watch Trump engage in this targeting and retaliation of any kind of free speech he doesn't like to hear," Schumer told reporters on Friday.

Carr said on Thursday that he is "not going anywhere" and vowed to continue his work taking on media firms and defending the "public interest."

data.title: Nymex Overview: Oil, Refined Product Contracts Trading Lower — OPIS
data.description:
Crude-oil and refined product contracts were trading lower as market participants appeared unfazed by a European Union plan to halt imports of Russian LNG next year.

The more active Nymex November West Texas Intermediate contract was off by about 70cts to $62.55/bbl on Friday and the October WTI contract was off by 65cts to $62.95/bbl.

The ICE November Brent contract was 70cts lower at $66.75/bbl and December Brent was down by 80cts to $66.10/bbl.

Both oil benchmarks are on track to finish lower for a third straight day, under pressure from uncertain global demand even as global output continues to edge higher.

Kaja Kallas, the EU's foreign policy chief, said in a social media post that the bloc plans to advance by one year the effective date of its ban on imports of Russian LNG. The post comes after the U.K. government last week issued new sanctions against Russia, including on dozens of its oil-carrying vessels.

Some analysts, however, said previous EU sanctions on Russia have mostly failed to reduce Russian oil exports.

Petroleum contracts were also pressured by a stronger dollar, which rose by about 0.3% against a basket of major foreign currencies. A stronger U.S. currency makes dollar-denominated crude contracts more expensive for non-U.S. investors.

In U.S. cash markets, Los Angeles and San Francisco spot CARBOB prices were trading 2-5cts/gal higher, while refined products in other markets largely mirrored moves in futures.

data.title: London stocks end event-packed week lower amid fiscal worries
data.description:
Key points:
  • FTSE 100 down 0.12%, FTSE 250 down 0.63%
  • Most major brokerages see no more BoE cuts this year
  • IG Group buys Australian crypto exchange, shares drop
  • Spire Healthcare jump, company mulls sale

London stocks ended an eventful week lower on Friday after a report showed Britain's borrowing had exceeded official forecasts, dampening consumer sentiment amid fiscal uncertainty.

The blue-chip FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 closed down 0.12%, registering a marginal weekly decline on looming worries around inflation. The mid-cap FTSE 250 fell 0.63% and also recorded a weekly loss.

Britain's borrowing has shot past the official forecasts that underpin the government's tax and spending plans, compounding the already big challenge facing finance minister Rachel Reeves in her November budget.

A survey showed on Friday that Britons turned more downbeat this month, with the looming prospect of tax hikes threatening to further dent consumer confidence.

Sterling fell and was the worst performer among G10 currencies, mirroring investors' concerns that Reeves may not be able to keep her budget under control.

UK retail sales for August showed a modest improvement, but broader economic concerns kept traders cautious.

The Bank of England's widely expected pause on Thursday followed a quarter-point cut in August, as it navigates sticky inflation, an uncertain growth and jobs outlook.

Major brokerages including Goldman Sachs, Citigroup and JPMorgan expect no more interest rate cuts by the BoE this year after the British central bank kept its key rate on hold.

Earlier this week, central banks in the U.S., Canada and Norway cut interest rates, boosting hopes of more easing to come. The Fed's 25 basis-point rate cut lifted riskier assets in the previous session.

Media stocks (.FTNMX403010) fell the most among sectors, down 2.2%, with WPP Plc declining 5.2%.

However, gains in precious metal miners helped stem losses in the FTSE 100, up 5%, as gold prices moved higher.

Fresnillo , Hochschild and Endeavour Mining gained between 5.2% and 4.5%.

Among individual stocks, IG Group said it had acquired Australian cryptocurrency exchange Independent Reserve for A$178 million ($117.4 million). Shares of the online trading platform declined 2.9%.

Spire Healthcare jumped 14.1% after the private hospital group said it was exploring strategic options, including a potential sale of the company.

data.title: Miran Won't Explain His Dissent in Writing, Breaking With Fed Tradition — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Matt Peterson

Stephen Miran, the new Federal Reserve governor appointed by President Donald Trump, won't issue a standard written dissent explaining his interest-rate vote at this week's meeting — a notable break with tradition.

On Wednesday, Miran argued for a bigger cut, half of a percentage point; the other 11 members of the rate-setting committee, including Chair Jerome Powell, voted for a quarter-point reduction. That cut was the Fed's first since 2024.

"A written dissent would be too short. I want to give full accounting for my views," Miran told Barron's.

Miran will speak Monday at the Economic Club of New York and plans to detail his arguments then.

Dissents have become rare on the Fed's board. They are typically explained in short written documents released after Fed meeting.

Trump appointed Miran to a temporary seat that came open this summer. That term expires in January, and Miran probably will have only three more votes during his time as a Fed member, though he may hold his seat if a replacement isn't chosen by then.

Miran's appointment comes as Trump seeks to gain control of the central bank's independent board.

A court on Monday ruled that Lisa Cook, also a Fed governor, may stay on the board while courts hear her challenge to Trump's decision to fire her over allegations that she committed mortgage fraud. She denies the charges.

Write to Matt Peterson at matt.peterson@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Miran Hopes to Convince FOMC to Cut Rates Faster — Market Talk
data.description:
1234 ET - Bond markets show little reaction to Fed Governor Miran's first public remarks about his dissent at the FOMC meeting, with the 10-year yield hovering around 4.14%. Miran said on CNBC he doubts tariffs will keep inflation hot, while a substantial decline in immigration could cool off housing prices. The Fed cut rates by 25 basis points this week, while Miran proposed a 50-bp trim. "I was sworn in...just about an hour before the meeting started," he said. "I'll be making my arguments in coming weeks and months and I hope that I'll be able to persuade some of my colleagues." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1218 ET - Canada intends to release Friday its own notice kickstarting consultations with businesses, workers and other levels of government regarding the U.S. review of USMCA, the formal trade treaty incorporating the big three North American economies. The U.S. kicked off the USMCA review process this week, ahead of the formal review of the trade treaty on July 1. Analysts expect the review to turn into another round of high-stakes negotiations, with the Trump administration likely pushing for more concessions from Mexico and Canada. Dominic LeBlanc, the Canadian minister in charge of U.S. trade relations, says the government seeks input on how to approach the USMCA review from those "affected by the recent turbulence in the trading relationship," and what specifically Canada should pursue in possible negotiations. (paul.vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

1153 ET - BlackBerry should be able to achieve or beat its guidance in its fiscal 2Q, CIBC's Todd Coupland says in a report. He says BlackBerry's average surprise compared with FactSet is 7% for revenue and almost triple for adjusted EBITDA since John Giamatteo took the helm as CEO at the end of 2023. "We expect this encouraging trend to continue," he says, noting thatshow progress in secure communications, its secure messaging and cybersecurity platform for governments and enterprises, as well as QNX, which is an embedded operating system that is used in automotive, medical and industrial applications. "We see these trends accelerating throughout F2026," Coupland adds. BlackBerry reports its 2Q on Sept. 25. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1148 ET - Greater adoption of artificial intelligence tools is the leading trend in travel, with the number of consumers who used an AI-based app to help with trip planning more than doubling year-over-year to about 30% in 2025, Wedbush Securities analysts say in a note after attending a panel with industry executives. While current experimentation of agentic AI in the travel industry is widespread, most companies are using the same undifferentiated AI chatbots, which have yet to materialize into any economic benefit, the analysts say. Companies should be able to monetize AI in the future with vertical use cases, like automating procurement or reimagining call centers, they say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1116 ET - Cracker Barrel abandoned its logo change and restaurant redesign, but other elements of the company's transformation plan will be leaned into more heavily, Benchmark analyst Todd Brooks says in a research note. Those elements have been working and can drive both increased traffic and customer frequency as well as stronger profitability, the analyst says. Management has already teased that it will double down on its menu innovation efforts, which have proven successful for the brand, Brooks says. The company will also continue to work on back-of-house efficiency efforts and drive further impact from the brand's 9 million-member loyalty program, whose members drive more than a third of Cracker Barrel's revenue, the analyst says. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1105 ET - Cracker Barrel Old Country Store is going to have to use additional marketing and discounting efforts to drive disgruntled customers back to the brand or find new ones, Benchmark analyst Todd Brooks says in a research note. Since the company tried to change its logo and update its branding in mid-August, customer pushback has caused traffic trends to fall from being down 1% in the first half of the year to being down 8% currently, the analyst says. The challenge now will be defining how long this traffic loss lasts after the company's hard pivot back to embracing its nostalgic branding and restaurant design, the analyst says. Shares fall 2% to $44.86. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1048 ET - Kohl's shares are poised to continue recovering from a multi-decade low in April as it faces easing year-over-year comparisons in the back half of the fiscal year, Citi analysts say in a research note. Web traffic data shows some acceleration in 3Q, leading the analysts to raise their comparable sales forecast. Analysts say they also have greater confidence in the company's ability to generate free cash flow. Kohl's doesn't have any major debt maturities until 2030. While 2H will present some macro challenges and longer-term secular issues remain, near-term upside and high short interest in the stock should move shares higher in the near term, the analysts say. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1007 ET - Citi analysts keep a "less-bearish-than-consensus" view of oil prices, seeing Brent easing toward $60 a barrel by the end of this year and into 2026. The OPEC+ decision to start unwinding a second layer of output cuts adds to expected global stock builds, which Citi now pegs at 1.1 million barrels a day in 2025 and 2.1 million b/d in 2026. The bank's bear case that could push Brent toward $50 could come about if global demand erodes on weaker macroeconomics and trade, supply rises faster than expected, "and if China stockpiling disappoints," they say in their quarterly outlook. The low-probability bull case with Brent above $75 would be driven by geopolitical events such as attacks on Russian infrastructure or an Israel-Iran escalation. Brent is off 0.7% at $66.97 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0948 ET - Cruise lines appear to be in for a mixed second half of the year, Truist analyst C. Patrick Scholes says. Scholes expects cruise companies not to disappoint with 3Q earnings and to maintain full-year guides, but he also sees an uptick in promotions and price cuts that could make it difficult for companies to beat expectations. At the start of the year, Scholes had been expecting outperformance. He now thinks growth rates are normalizing, based on booking and pricing data. "We would describe our latest observations as having 'mixed implications' for the industry/stocks," he says. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

-0

0939 ET - Only five of the Fed's 12 reserve-bank presidents vote on policy each year, and in 2025, the Cleveland Fed's Beth Hammack is not a voter. But the reserve banks submit input on how the Fed should adjust its discount lending rate, which typically moves in concert with its main benchmark interest-rate target. At Wednesday's meeting, the Cleveland Fed was one of the only reserve banks not to request that the discount rate be lowered alongside the fed funds target range. Bill Nelson of the Bank Policy Institute suggests this can be read as a "shadow dissent" from Hammack, who has been hawkish. "In this case, I infer that President Hammack would have preferred no change to the FOMC target," he writes. Hammack will be a voter again starting in January. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

0928 ET - Fed independence has become an even hotter topic with Governor Miran still holding his job at the White House while voting for an interest rate cut deeper than his FOMC colleagues supported. But Wall Street seems to have taken the change in stride, which Minneapolis Fed's Neel Kashkari attributes to confidence in U.S. institutions. "There's widespread appreciation for how important Fed independence is," among investors and lawmakers, he says on CNBC. "I think people are betting on the institutions of the country continuing to keep Fed independence, to keep it outside of the short-term political process." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0915 ET - Oil futures are lower as the EU unveils a new package of sanctions against Russia that targets banks, oil vessels and buyers of Russian oil, while lowering the crude oil price cap. Robert Yawger of Mizuho said this week that the previous 18 rounds of EU sanctions since the 2022 invasion of Ukraine "have proven largely ineffective in stopping the flow of Russian oil exports." Ukrainian attacks on Russian oil facilities lifted prices early this week. WTI is down 0.5% at $63.26 a barrel and Brent is off 0.4% at $67.20. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Kate Spade New York Champions Women's Mental Health Through Style and Sport in Kigali, Rwanda
data.description:
Kate Spade New York Hosts Over 350 Women For Its Annual Regional Women's Mental Health Gathering.

NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / September 19, 2025 / At kate spade new york, mental health is recognized as a cornerstone of empowering women and girls around the world. In alignment with this belief, over the summer, the brand hosted its annual Women's Mental Health event in Kigali, Rwanda, and - with its signature kate spade joyful twist - brought together over 350 attendees to celebrate the powerful intersection of women's mental health, sport, and fashion.

On Purpose is the brand's social enterprise initiative. As part of On Purpose, kate spade has been funding women's empowerment work in Masoro, Rwanda since 2013 in collaboration with Abahizi CBC, a local handbag production facility. As the partnership began, and as the program grew, the need for empowerment and mental health resources within the community became increasingly clear.

Its mission is to use the On Purpose supply chain to empower the women who make these collections, and in turn invest in the communities they call home. Through this work, kate spade has seen that investing in a woman's mental health positively impacts her voice, choice, and power.

Hosted in partnership with Abahizi Rwanda and Issoko Community Development, this year's Women's Mental Health event titled "Her Mind. Her Game. Her Style.", took place at the newly constructed sports and entertainment venue, Zaria Court Kigali. The gathering featured a powerful lineup of speakers:

  • The Honorable Nelly Mukazayire, Minister of Sport​, Government of Rwanda

  • Clare Akamanzi, CEO, NBA Africa ​

  • Norette Turimuci, Kate Spade Social Impact Council​

  • Stacy Charlène Usanase, APR Women's BBC

  • Taryn Bird, Executive Director, Social Impact, Kate Spade New York

kate spade new york has set a global goal to give 250,000 women and girls access to mental health resources by 2030. The brand will host its annual Global Summit on Women's Mental Health in New York City on Friday, October 10th, aligning with World Mental Health Day.

Learn more about kate spade's social impact work here.

data.title: Judge strikes Trump's lawsuit against New York Times over its content
data.description:
A federal judge on Friday struck Donald Trump's $15 billion defamation lawsuit against the New York Times over its content, calling it a "decidedly improper" effort to attack an adversary.

U.S. District Judge Steven Merryday in Tampa, Florida said Trump's complaint violated a federal civil procedure rule requiring a short and plain statement of why he should prevail.

A complaint should "fairly, precisely, directly, soberly, and economically inform the defendants ... of the nature and content of the claims," Merryday wrote. "A complaint is not a public forum for vituperation and invective--not a protected platform to rage against an adversary."

Merryday gave Trump 28 days to file an amended complaint of no more than 40 pages. The White House and Trump's lawyers did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
